Barack Obama:
Biography of Barack Hussein Obama

Birth and Early Life:
Barack Hussein Obama II (born 4 August, 1961) is an American politician, who served as the 44th President of the United States of America from 2009 to 2017. He was born in Honolulu, which is the capital of the American state of Hawaii. His father was from Kenya, while mother was American.

Education:
Obama obtained a Bachelor of Arts degree in political science from Columbia University in 1983. Later he entered Harvard Law School and there became the first black president of the Harvard Law Review.

Work Life and Political Career:
From 1988 to 1991 Obama worked as a community organizer in Chicago. After this he gave service as Illinois state senator from 1997 to 2004. From 2005 to 2008 he worked as the American senator of Illinois.

Service as President:
In the 2008 election Obama contested for the post of president under the Democratic Party and won. His presidential period has an important place in the history of America, in which he made his identity as the first African American as American president. During his service he gave special attention to the American economy, world peace and world health policies.

Place in History:
Barack Obama became the first African American president in American history, whose important contribution remained for diversity and equality in American society. During his presidential period, for his important contribution of leadership for the political and social development of America, he became a world famous leader.

Xi Jinping:
Biography of Xi Jinping

Birth and Early Life
Xi Jinping (born 15 June 1953) is a Chinese politician who is the fifth highest leader of the People’s Republic of China. Born as the eldest son from the second marriage of Xi Zhongxun and Qi Xin, Xi Jinping is known as a “princeling” (prince ling), but he maintained a low profile in his early career.

Adolescence and Impact of the Cultural Revolution
During Xi Jinping’s adolescence, his father Xi Zhongxun was politically removed, due to which he was sent in 1969 during the Cultural Revolution to Liangjiahe village in Shaanxi province. He lived there in a yaodong, where he spent the early stage of his life trying to join the Chinese Communist Party (CCP) after several unsuccessful attempts. During this period, he served as a local party secretary, which helped in developing his political thinking and leadership skills.

Emerging Political Power
During Xi Jinping’s rise in politics, he showed determination and strong leadership in the Chinese Communist Party. In this phase of his life, he played an important role in the development of political policies and policies based on his experience.

Important Appointments and Leadership
Since 2012, Xi Jinping has been serving as the general secretary of the Chinese Communist Party (CCP) and chairman of the party’s Central Military Commission (CMC). Since 2013, he has been the president of China as well as chairman of the state Central Military Commission. Through these appointments, he has started a new phase for China’s political, economic, and military leadership.
